title: Settings

section.inferenceDesc: Local Ollama connection and the model used for answers.
section.runtimeDesc: Python interpreter and database path used to launch MCP servers.
section.agentDesc: Tune model sampling and the tool-call limit, and inspect the actual system prompt.
section.appearanceDesc: Accessibility display settings: font size and high contrast (theme and language are in the top-right header).

appearance.highContrastHint: Increases the contrast of borders, secondary text, and focus rings for readability.
appearance.instantNote: Changes apply immediately and are saved automatically.

agent.temperatureHint: 0–1: lower is more deterministic, follows the tool-call format better, and fabricates less. Clinical default 0.3.
agent.maxTurnsHint: 1–20: the cap on consecutive tool-call rounds per answer, to avoid endless loops. Default 12.
agent.systemPromptHint: This prompt is assembled automatically and injected with the live tool list and the 'no fabricated tables/tools' rules, applied before every request.

desc: Settings are saved to config.json in the project folder. Changes automatically reconnect all MCP servers.

help.ollamaUrl: Address of the local Ollama service. To keep data on‑device, only localhost / 127.0.0.1 are allowed; non‑local URLs are blocked.
help.model: The local model used to answer and call tools; it must support tool calling. Use "Detect" to list installed models — stronger models call tools more reliably.
help.pythonPath: The Python interpreter used to launch the four MCP server subprocesses. Use an environment that has the mcp and pydantic packages installed (a venv's python path works).
help.dbPath: Path to the MIMIC notes SQLite database, passed to servers that need it via the MIMIC_DB_PATH env var. Build it from NOTEEVENTS.csv with mcp/scripts/build_db.py.
help.temperature: Controls output randomness. At each step the model samples from the next‑token probability distribution; before softmax the scores are divided by temperature T — lower T sharpens the distribution (more deterministic, closer to picking the top token), higher T flattens it (more creative). The name comes from the Boltzmann distribution in physics (temperature = randomness). Clinical default 0.3 for stable, reproducible answers.
help.maxTurns: The cap on consecutive tool rounds (call → see result → call again) per answer. Too low may cut a query short; too high can drag on and waste resources when the model loops. Default 12 balances depth with avoiding infinite loops.
help.systemPrompt: The top‑level instruction auto‑assembled and injected to the model before every request (read‑only). It enforces rules like "the only way to get data is to emit a tool call" and "never fabricate tables/tools", plus the live tool list. Shown for transparency.
help.fontSize: Scales all text app‑wide (via the root rem size); icons are unaffected. An accessibility setting that applies immediately and is saved automatically.

ollamaUrl.label: Ollama URL
ollamaUrl.hint: Local addresses only (localhost / 127.0.0.1). Data must not be sent to any third party.

model.label: Model name
model.hint: Must support tool calling, e.g. qwen2.5, gpt-oss:20b.

pythonPath.label: Python path
pythonPath.hint: The Python interpreter used to launch all MCP servers.

dbPath.label: SQLite database path
dbPath.hint: Passed to each MCP server via MIMIC_DB_PATH (servers that don't need a database ignore it).

localWarn: Must be a local address, otherwise it will be blocked.
mcpSectionDesc: Each server is an independent Python stdio subprocess. You can enable/disable, edit the name and script path, and add/remove custom servers.
mcpBuiltinHint: Built-in servers can be disabled but not removed (they're re-added on next launch).
mcpInvalid: Each server needs a name and at least a command or a script path.
mcpPending: Not connected yet; save to connect
mcpStandardHint: Uses the standard MCP launch format (command/args/env — the same as Claude Desktop / mcp.json).
mcpImportFail: JSON parse failed or wrong shape (needs an mcpServers object).

model.customPlaceholder: Enter a model name, e.g. gpt-oss:20b
model.noModels: No models detected. Install one with ollama pull first.
model.detectedCount_one: Detected {{count}} local model; choose "Custom…" to type manually.
model.detectedCount_other: Detected {{count}} local models; choose "Custom…" to type manually.

test.ollamaModelMissing: (⚠️ the configured model "{{model}}" is not in the list)
test.dbMissing: Not found: {{path}}. Build it with mcp/scripts/build_db.py or fix the path.
test.mcpNone: No enabled MCP server, or reconnection failed.

save.ok: Saved and reconnected: {{connected}}/{{total}} servers connected, {{tools}} tools total.
save.partial: Saved, but no server connected successfully. Please check the paths.
